title: Critical-weight tangentiality
summary: Proves algebraically that Δ̃ᵏ preserves boundary values at weight k − n/2, then assembles the canonical abstract GJMS operator family.

mathematicalContent.heading: Boundary values modulo Q
mathematicalContent.body: Two homogeneous ambient extensions represent the same boundary density when their difference is Qh. Because Q has homogeneity two, h has weight k−n/2−2 when f and g have the critical input weight.

mathematicalContent.heading: Critical coefficient cancellation
mathematicalContent.body: The iterated commutator formula expresses Δ̃ᵏ(Qh) as QΔ̃ᵏh plus a multiple of Δ̃ᵏ⁻¹h. At the critical error weight the scalar coefficient is zero, leaving a Q-multiple.

mathematicalContent.heading: Descent to conformal densities
mathematicalContent.body: Linearity then gives Δ̃ᵏf−Δ̃ᵏg = Δ̃ᵏ(Qh), which vanishes modulo Q. Thus Δ̃ᵏ is tangential at the critical weight and defines the canonical abstract operator of order 2k.
mathematicalContent.formula: P_{2k}[f]:=\left.\widetilde\Delta^k\widetilde f\right|_{Q=0},\qquad P_{2k}:\mathcal E\!\left[k-\frac n2\right]\to\mathcal E\!\left[-k-\frac n2\right].

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Constructs the basic witness that every function of the form Qh is zero modulo Q.
correspondence.leanType: theorem vanishesModQ_Qmul_local (h : Function Conf) : CalConf.VanishesModQ (CalConf.Qmul h)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Converts vanishing of f−g modulo Q into equality of the boundary values represented by f and g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem sameBoundaryValue_of_difference_vanishes {f g : Function Conf} (h : CalConf.VanishesModQ (fun U => f U - g U)) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This is the key cancellation lemma. If h has weight k−n/2−2, the non-Q term in the iterated Laplacian–Q commutator has coefficient zero, so Δ̃ᵏ(Qh) is itself a Q-multiple.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_Qmul_vanishes_at_criticalWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {h : Function Conf} (hh : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusInputWeight CalConf k - 2) h) : CalConf.VanishesModQ (CalConf.lapPow k (CalConf.Qmul h))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For homogeneous f and g of critical weight with f−g=Qh and h of the required error weight, linearity and the key lemma show that their kth Laplacian powers still differ by a Q-multiple.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_preserves_sameBoundaryValue_at_criticalWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {f g : Function Conf} (_hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) f) (_hg : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) g) (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValueAtWeight (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (CalConf.lapPow k f) (CalConf.lapPow k g)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Main theorem: under AlgebraicIdentities, the kth ambient Laplacian power is tangential at weight k−n/2. Hence its boundary value is independent of the chosen homogeneous extension.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_isTangentialAtCriticalWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Uses the proved tangentiality theorem, together with the weight theorem from Ambientconstruction, to define the canonical abstract GJMS operator associated with Δ̃ᵏ.
correspondence.leanType: def canonicalAmbientGJMS (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical operator constructed from the kth ambient power stores index k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_k (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).k = k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical kth operator has expected differential order 2k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_order (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).order = 2 * k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forgetting its weight and tangentiality data returns the ambient operator Δ̃ᵏ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_toOperator (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator = ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Applying the underlying ambient operator of the canonical package to f is exactly lapPow k f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator f = CalConf.lapPow k f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical operator carries the critical input homogeneity to the GJMS output homogeneity.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_mapsWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.MapsHomogeneousWeight (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) (calculusOutputWeight CalConf k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Restates for the canonical package the proved independence of homogeneous extension.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_tangential (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages all canonical ambient operators, one for every k, into a Family.
Selecting index k from the canonical family returns the canonical operator constructed from Δ̃ᵏ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily_get (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily CalConf H).get k = canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The kth member of the canonical family has order 2k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily_get_order (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : ((canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily CalConf H).get k).order = 2 * k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At k=0 the canonical ambient operator is the identity.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_zero_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H 0).toOperator f = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At k=1 the underlying canonical ambient operator is one application of the ambient Laplacian.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_one_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H 1).toOperator f = CalConf.lap f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At k=2 the underlying canonical ambient operator is the second ambient Laplacian power.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_two_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H 2).toOperator f = CalConf.lapPow 2 f

references.note: Original construction of conformally invariant powers of the Laplacian.

references.locator: Ambient Laplacian and homogeneous extension discussion
references.note: Ambient-metric background for the formal construction.

limitations: The tangentiality proof is Lean-verified relative to the abstract Calculus.AlgebraicIdentities laws, including linearity and the iterated Laplacian–Q commutator. The file does not construct those laws from a concrete Fefferman–Graham ambient metric, address even-dimensional existence restrictions for arbitrary k, or derive the usual lower-order curvature formulas for GJMS operators.
